# Manifest

> Manifest is a declarative domain modeling language and runtime. It compiles `.manifest` source files to a deterministic Intermediate Representation (IR) that enforces guards, policies, constraints, and events identically on every platform.

Manifest is designed for AI agents that emit, validate, and reason about business rules as code. The runtime is IR-first: the compiled IR is the executable contract, not generated TypeScript.

## Get Started

- [Introduction](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/introduction): What Manifest is, how the IR-first model works, and the two integration patterns (projections vs embedded runtime).
- [Quickstart](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/quickstart): Install @angriff36/manifest, write your first .manifest file, compile to IR, and run a command in under five minutes.
- [Installation](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/installation): Add @angriff36/manifest to any Node.js 18+ project; configure TypeScript paths and verify the CLI.

## Language

- [Entities](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/language/entities): Define typed data structures with properties, computed values, relationships, concurrency controls, constraints, and transitions.
- [Enums](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/language/enums): Declare reusable enum types with optional display labels and ordinals; reference them from entity properties just like built-in scalars.
- [Commands](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/language/commands): Declare named operations that enforce guards and policies, execute ordered actions (mutate, emit, compute), and return structured CommandResult.
- [Guards and Policies](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/language/guards-policies): Use guards for precondition checks and policies for authorization. Both are boolean expressions evaluated against runtime context.
- [Events](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/language/events): Declare typed events with channels and payload schemas. Commands emit them via the emit action; subscribe with runtime.onEvent().
- [Stores](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/language/stores): Declare where each entity's instances are persisted. Targets: memory, localStorage, postgres, supabase, durable, custom.
- [Computed Properties](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/language/computed-properties): Declare derived values with the computed keyword. Evaluated against current instance state with dependency tracking and cycle detection.

## Integration

- [Integration Overview](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/integration/overview): Choose between projections (generated code) and the embedded runtime (direct engine use). When to use each.
- [Projections](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/integration/projections): How projections read compiled IR and emit platform-specific artifacts. Available projections: Next.js, Prisma, Routes.
- [Embedded Runtime](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/integration/embedded-runtime): Instantiate RuntimeEngine in your own handlers. Full execution pipeline, CommandResult shape, and RuntimeOptions reference.
- [Next.js Projection](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/integration/nextjs): Generate App Router route handlers, TypeScript types, and a typed client SDK from Manifest IR.
- [Prisma Projection](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/integration/prisma): Generate a schema.prisma file from Manifest IR. Supports composite PKs/FKs, referential actions, decimal precision, indexes, and dbAttributes.

## Adapters

- [Custom Stores](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/adapters/custom-stores): Implement the Store interface and provide it via storeProvider to persist entity instances in any database.
- [Audit Sink](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/adapters/audit-sink): Wire in an AuditSink to receive one AuditRecord per runCommand call — success or failure — for observability.
- [Outbox](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/adapters/outbox): Use OutboxStore to persist emitted events for at-least-once delivery with transactional guarantees.

## CLI

- [CLI Overview](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/cli/overview): The Manifest CLI compiles .manifest files to IR, generates code via projections, and validates IR against the schema.
- [CLI Commands](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/cli/commands): Full syntax and options for every CLI command: compile, generate, build, validate, check, scan, config, and more.
- [Governance Commands](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/cli/governance): enforce-surface, integration-check, audit-governance, audit-bypasses — enforce write boundaries and audit compliance.
- [Configuration](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/cli/configuration): Full reference for manifest.config.yaml: src globs, IR output, projection options (nextjs, prisma, routes).

## Help

- [FAQ](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/faq): Common questions about Manifest's design, architecture, integration patterns, and comparison to other tools.
- [Troubleshooting](https://manifest-b1e8623f.mintlify.app/troubleshooting): Diagnose and fix common errors when compiling, running commands, or integrating with Next.js and Prisma.
